structure_y_bands.30-94: open eyes pair (top, ignored - duplicates existing 'open')

source.copainter_alpha_quirk: max alpha across ZIP is ~254, never 255; pixel content preserved as-is

jack_eye_left_closed_r1.png.represents: character LEFT eye, image-LEFT in layer_43 - sleepy/heavy-lidded squint

jack_eye_right_closed_r1.png.represents: character RIGHT eye, image-RIGHT in layer_43 - sleepy/heavy-lidded squint

half_variants_rejected.reason: Top eye pair in layer_43 (y=30-94) is Jack's normal OPEN deadpan look - matches existing jack_eye_*_open.png in style; CoPainter does NOT add a distinct half-state worth staging. Bottom pair was already taken as 'closed' for r1.

naming_convention.rule: filenames use CHARACTER anatomy (left=character left=image-LEFT in this layer's frame)
naming_convention.note: layer_43 internally already shows left/right as character-anatomy correct

composite_anchor_seethrough.scale_basis: CoPainter head width 253 px maps to See-through face width 87 px -> 0.3439

composite_anchor_production_head.scale_basis: rough match — eye crop 60 px wide -> target ~80 px on production head

do_not: modify pixel content (crops are byte-identical pixel extractions of the source region)
promote to assets/puppet/layers/eyes/ without a fresh QC comparing the squint look to the existing 'closed' full-lid-down convention
rename or recolour - keep CoPainter's native semi-alpha edges
